response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack/web developer with 15+ years of experience, but the overlap with this role's requirements is minimal. The position demands native-level Japanese proficiency — an absolute hard requirement — which the candidate does not possess. The role is centered on C programming for embedded networking firmware with security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Linux kernel internals), whereas the candidate's core stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and web frameworks. The only C/C++ exposure on the resume is a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. There is no evidence of networking, security protocols, switch architecture, or embedded firmware experience relevant to LAN-switch product development.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) demonstrating consistent delivery across diverse domains and languages, including some low-level work (C/C++, DLang, embedded robotic arms).
Demonstrated experience with Linux environments and low-latency systems (async I/O, high-performance databases at Sociomantic), showing some systems-level awareness.
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality) that would be valuable on any engineering team.
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement for the role.
No experience with security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, or network switch architecture.
C programming exposure is negligible (6 months); the candidate's entire career trajectory is web/full-stack, making embedded firmware ownership unrealistic in the near term.
